title: URIs, URL Resolution, Origins, DNS, and Destination Identity · Go Client Systems · Program Defense
summary: A release board evaluating a changed infrastructure-provider contract must defend independent decisions across fresh cases, including one ambiguous failure and one changed boundary for uris, url resolution, origins, dns, and destination identity, producing destination-resolution and origin contract. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a Go 1.26 net/http destination-resolution and origin contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case…
objectives: Parse scheme, authority, host, port, path, query, and fragment and resolve relative references against the correct base. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Encode path segments and query names or values at the correct structural layer without double encoding. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Compare origins from scheme, host, and effective port while separating URI authority, Host, and connection address. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go Uri Components Resolution · changed case · Quiz Uri Components Resolution
steps.instruction:
quiz case — Go vendor-release candidate receives base https://api3.example.test/v1/items/ and reference ../reports?q=a%2Fb. Resolve it structurally, compare origin and authority, trace two DNS answers, and reject any connected destination outside the approved range. Competency probe: Parse scheme, authority, host, port, path, query, and fragment and resolve relative references against the correct base. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Which action demonstrates Uri Components Resolution without dropping earlier contracts?
